title: cola lepidota
intro: Cola lepidota, also known as the yellow cola or kola nut tree, is a species of plant in the family Sterculiaceae. It is a deciduous tree native to tropical Africa, with edible seeds that have been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The seeds of Cola lepidota contain medicinal properties such as caffeine and theobromine, which have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, headache, and indigestion. The seeds can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder for use in herbal remedies, and have been reported to have health benefits such as improving cognitive function and boosting energy levels.
